commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And Absalom made Amasa captain of the host instead of Joab: which Amasa was a man's son, whose name was Ithra an Israelite, that went in to Abigail the daughter of Nahash, sister to Zeruiah Joab's mother. Jazrael, or Israel. (Du Hamel) Gortius would read Ismael, as 1 Paralipomenon ii. 17, Jet her, the Ismaelite. (Haydock) Went in. This expressing makes Sanchez believe that Amasa was illegitimate. (Menochius) Naas is either the name of Isai's wife, or rather the latter had both names, 1 Paralipomenon ii. Septuagint read Jesse, in the edition of Complut. ; in others, Naas. (Calmet) Joab; so that these two were own cousins, and both nephews of David.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
